generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance CarbonDioxideSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded within BIM models to capture runtime CO2 levels for operational monitoring and dynamic synchronization with physical infrastructure."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CO2DataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded within BIM models to capture runtime temperature data for operational monitoring and dynamic synchronization with physical infrastructure."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded within BIM models to capture occupancy data for operational monitoring and dynamic synchronization with physical infrastructure."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded within BIM models to capture runtime humidity levels for operational monitoring and dynamic synchronization with physical infrastructure."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance LightLevelSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded within BIM models to capture ambient light levels for operational monitoring and dynamic synchronization with physical infrastructure."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightLevelDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance CO2DataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CarbonDioxideSensor
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance LightLevelDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightLevelSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric representation of physical building structures and spatial relationships inherited from BIM, serving as the foundational layer for DT deployment."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler, SemanticEnrichmentLayer
]

instance BcsBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic control logic and operational states for sensors, actuators, and controllers defined by Building Control Engineers, encapsulating BCS component behaviors."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler, SemanticEnrichmentLayer
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated sensor telemetry and actuator command data collected during deployment, representing the real-time operational state of the physical building."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CO2DataTransmitted, TemperatureDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted, LightLevelDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeDataExchangeEnabler
]

instance BimStaticMetadata : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Static metadata extracted from BIM models representing architectural, MEP, and structural components, serving as the baseline for DT deployment."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimIntegrationLayer
]

instance EnrichedBimModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Semantically enriched BIM models combining static BIM data with dynamic BCS component definitions, ready for automated DT artifact generation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData BimStaticMetadata, BcsBehavioralModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler
]

instance DeployableDtArtifacts : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Automatically generated DT components (simulation models, control logic modules) compatible with downstream DT operations, produced via MDE-driven transformations."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnrichedBimModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CustomServiceDevelopmentEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Applies MDE principles to extract structured representations from enriched BIM models and automatically generate deployable DT artifacts, bridging static BIM and dynamic DT requirements."
    DTDFVocab:enables AutomatedDtArtifactGenerationService, DeviceModelingService
]

instance SemanticEnrichmentLayer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Adds dynamic behavior models (sensor/actuator logic) to static BIM data, enabling real-time operational synchronization between physical and digital representations."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataExchangeService, DeviceModelingService
]

instance BimIntegrationLayer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Facilitates direct mapping of physical BCS components to digital twins and automated transformation of BIM data into DT-ready formats, serving as the foundational data source."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceModelingService, AutomatedDtArtifactGenerationService
]

instance RealTimeDataExchangeEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Ensures continuous bidirectional synchronization between physical systems and the DT using high-frequency, low-latency protocols, enabling live monitoring and dynamic updates."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataExchangeService, InteractiveVisualizationService
]

instance CustomServiceDevelopmentEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Provides standardized extension points and plugin SDKs/APIs for integrating custom services (analytics, optimization algorithms) into the DT framework."
    DTDFVocab:enables CustomServiceDevelopmentService, PredictiveOptimizationService
]

instance ModularArchitectureFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Features standardized extension points for integrating custom services/plugins while maintaining interoperability with legacy BCS systems, enabling evolutionary adaptation."
]

// C6: Services
instance DeviceModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables semantic modeling of BCS components (sensors, actuators, controllers) within BIM, addressing static representation limitations and providing unified semantic foundations for DT transformations."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviceConfigurationInsight, ControlLogicDefinitionAction
]

instance RealTimeDataExchangeService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Ensures bidirectional, continuous synchronization between physical building and digital twin, enabling live monitoring of system states and automated updates based on operational deviations."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateMonitoringInsight, DynamicUpdateAction
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides immersive interfaces (3D BIM-integrated dashboards, web/AR/VR platforms) for real-time system monitoring and operational control (e.g., HVAC adjustments, actuator triggering)."
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveMonitoringInsight, OperationalControlAction
]

instance CustomServiceDevelopmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Allows extension of DT functionality through modular plugins for analytics, predictive maintenance, or optimization, with runtime integration of third-party modules."
    DTDFVocab:provides CustomAnalyticsInsight, PluginIntegrationAction
]

instance AutomatedDtArtifactGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automatically generates deployable DT artifacts from enriched BIM models with minimal manual intervention, leveraging MDE to produce standardized, interoperable components."
    DTDFVocab:provides ArtifactGenerationInsight, DeploymentAutomationAction
]

instance PredictiveOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integrates third-party analytics tools (predictive maintenance, energy optimization) or bespoke algorithms for data-driven decision-making and system optimization."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationInsight, AutomatedAdjustmentAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance DeviceConfigurationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Semantic definitions of BCS components (sensors, actuators, controllers) within BIM, enabling precise mapping to digital twin representations."
]

instance ControlLogicDefinitionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Definition of control logic tied to physical devices, enabling automated operational behaviors in the digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SystemStateMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of sensor readings, actuator statuses, and environmental conditions for facility management."
]

instance DynamicUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated synchronization of digital twin with physical system changes (occupancy, environmental conditions)."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ImmersiveMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D BIM-integrated dashboards overlaying real-time DT data for spatial awareness and operational oversight."
]

instance OperationalControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "User-driven adjustments to HVAC setpoints, actuator triggering, and system parameter modifications."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance CustomAnalyticsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Integration of third-party analytics (predictive maintenance, energy optimization) for advanced decision support."
]

instance PluginIntegrationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Runtime integration of custom services and plugins to extend DT functionality."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ArtifactGenerationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured representations of deployable DT components (simulation models, control logic modules) generated via MDE."
]

instance DeploymentAutomationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated deployment of DT artifacts with minimal manual intervention."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Data-driven recommendations for system optimization based on predictive analytics and real-time performance metrics."
]

instance AutomatedAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated system adjustments based on optimization algorithms and predictive maintenance triggers."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System actuator for HVAC systems, receiving commands from the digital twin for environmental control."
]

instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System actuator for lighting systems, receiving commands from the digital twin for illumination control."
]

    base:desc "Building Control System actuator for security systems, receiving commands from the digital twin for access control and monitoring."
]

// C1: Environment
instance BuildingControlSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains CarbonDioxideSensor, TemperatureSensor, OccupancySensor, HumiditySensor, LightLevelSensor, HVACActuator, LightingActuator, SecurityActuator
]

instance DigitalTwinFrameworkEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains BimGeometricModel, BcsBehavioralModel, RealTimeOperationalData, BimStaticMetadata, EnrichedBimModel, DeployableDtArtifacts, ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler, SemanticEnrichmentLayer, BimIntegrationLayer, RealTimeDataExchangeEnabler, CustomServiceDevelopmentEnabler
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system facilitates **control flows from the virtual to physical domain** through the interaction of **Facility Managers**, who operate the deployed DT for real-time monitoring and control of physical infrastructure. Commands are transmitted from the DT Framework to physical systems via bidirectional data exchange, with the framework acting as a central orchestrator. While specific command types (e.g., HVAC adjustments, lighting control) are not enumerated, the system supports **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic adjustments without full redeployment. Validation and authentication mechanisms are not explicitly described, but the framework’s **interoperability requirements (NF_X)** imply robust mechanisms for ensuring secure and reliable command execution. Feedback systems are inferred through the **continuous synchronization** loop, where physical responses (e.g., sensor updates) are fed back into the DT for validation. Conflict resolution is not detailed, but the modular architecture suggests fault-tolerant design. Command execution confirmation is implied by the system’s emphasis on **real-time monitoring and control**, ensuring closed-loop operational integrity."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates on a **real-time synchronization paradigm**, enabling continuous data exchange between the physical asset (e.g., a building) and its virtual counterpart to ensure up-to-date insights for simulation, analysis, and optimization. While explicit synchronization frequencies or latency requirements are not specified, the system is designed to support **time-sensitive decision-making** across the entire infrastructure lifecycle, from design to operational adaptations. The temporal granularity is implicitly tied to the **runtime data integration**, which must align with the dynamic behavior of the physical asset to maintain fidelity in predictive analytics and operational optimization."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **cyber-physical architecture** that integrates **static models** (e.g., building designs) with **runtime data** to create a unified virtual representation. This orchestration follows a **modular, service-oriented platform** to enable life-cycle-encompassing optimization, from initial design to refurbishments and operational adaptations. The system leverages **carefully designed integration mechanisms** to ensure real-time insights, predictive analytics, and enhanced decision-making, though specific orchestration standards (e.g., Industry Foundation Classes) are not mentioned in the provided context."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define sensors, actuators, and controllers within the BIM environment, creating a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations. This phase leverages BIM’s static capabilities to model Building Control System (BCS) components.
  2. **Model Transformation**: The framework employs **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles** to extract structured representations from enriched BIM models, enabling **automated generation of deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed, establishing a **real-time synchronized** connection between the physical building and its digital twin. This phase ensures continuous bidirectional data exchange.
  4. **Operation and Monitoring**: Facility Managers interact with the DT to monitor system states and operate physical infrastructure, relying on **interactive visualization** for immersive interfaces.
  5. **Custom Service Integration**: DT Engineers extend functionality by developing and integrating **custom services and plugins** via standardized extension points, enabling the DT to evolve with operational needs.
  Transition management between phases is facilitated by the framework’s **modular architecture**, ensuring seamless progression from static BIM-based modeling to dynamic DT operations. Lifecycle governance is implicit in the framework’s adherence to **stakeholder-specific use cases** (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers) and the **automated, structured transformation pipeline** that minimizes manual errors and ensures consistency."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system evaluates model fidelity using the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**, which quantifies how closely the DT reflects its physical counterpart. Validation processes ensure the accuracy and reliability of the twin, though specific fidelity levels (e.g., LOD 300-500), verification tools (e.g., EnergyPlus), or uncertainty quantification methods (e.g., Kalman filtering) are not detailed. The framework emphasizes **model validation** as a core requirement, ensuring that the generated DT artifacts align with operational expectations. Error detection and correction mechanisms are implied through the real-time synchronization and modular architecture, but no explicit methodologies (e.g., consensus algorithms) are described."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **centralized network topology**, with the DT Framework serving as the primary hub for bidirectional data exchange between physical and digital components. The framework’s **modular architecture** includes **standardized extension points**, enabling integration with heterogeneous systems while adhering to **interoperability requirements (NF_X)**. While specific communication protocols (e.g., MQTT, OPC UA) are not named, the system leverages technologies like **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** (referenced in related work) to support scalable and reliable data transmission. Bandwidth and latency requirements are not quantified, but the emphasis on **real-time synchronization** implies low-latency, high-throughput connections. Reliability and redundancy mechanisms are inferred from the **scalability and maintainability requirements (NF_X)**, ensuring fault tolerance across infrastructure components. Security protocols are not explicitly detailed, but the framework’s design prioritizes **accessibility for users with varying technical expertise** and **interoperability across heterogeneous systems**, suggesting built-in safeguards for secure communication."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system faces challenges due to the **fragmented nature of models and data** in heterogeneous infrastructure environments, necessitating robust **interoperability frameworks**. While the document does not specify exact protocols (e.g., IFC, OPC UA, RESTful APIs) or external systems (e.g., CAFM), it highlights the need for **systematic integration** with external data sources and platforms. The system is designed to align with **open standards and modular architectures** (e.g., Eclipse Hono and Ditto) to facilitate seamless data exchange and collaboration across stakeholders, ensuring compatibility with broader enterprise systems and other Digital Twins."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to **AECO-specific standards** (e.g., BIM) to address the limitations of static BIM representations in dynamic DT environments. It introduces **standardized extension points** for modular service integration, enabling interoperability with third-party tools and analytics. While the document does not explicitly mention certification requirements, it references **interoperability standards** such as Eclipse Hono and Ditto for real-time data exchange, ensuring compatibility across heterogeneous systems. The proposal focuses on **standardizing BIM semantics** (e.g., R1 in Table III) to bridge the gap between static BIM models and dynamic DT operations. Compliance frameworks are implied through the framework’s alignment with **model-driven deployment principles** and its support for **automated, standardized workflows**, though specific compliance certifications (e.g., ISO, IEC) are not detailed."
]
